boundaryStatement: Confidence is not approval. Preliminary decision-support only. Final feasibility depends on base material, geometry, service conditions, inspection requirements, and expert review.
noBackendNote: No backend. No data sent by this site.
noAutomaticSendingNote: No automatic sending. Nothing is sent unless you send it from your own email client.

brief.situation: Public-safe dummy example: rough LMD repair RFQ.
brief.component: Industrial component not yet fully identified
brief.goal: Turn a vague RFQ into known facts, gaps, risk flags, evidence needs, and review route.
brief.damageOrBuildArea: Damage area mentioned but depth not defined.
riskFlags: Unknown material blocks firm repair recommendations.
Missing geometry and damage depth can change the route.
Downtime pressure should not remove inspection requirements.
brief.preliminaryRoute: Use the RFQ Prompt-to-Structure Converter before asking for feasibility.
brief.completenessNote: Completeness describes whether the brief can support a useful conversation. It is not feasibility, approval, or release evidence.
brief.evidenceBurdenNote: Evidence burden is a planning label, not release approval.
brief.nextAction: Ask for missing fields and prepare a compact Exafuse-ready review summary.
brief.exafuseReviewRoute: Use Exafuse for commercial and technical review after the RFQ facts and gaps are structured.
brief.boundaryStatement: Confidence is not approval. Preliminary decision-support only. Final feasibility depends on base material, geometry, service conditions, inspection requirements, and expert review.
